textByHashes.1becd9abecf8134f410b6f5886511d3b22fdb8a9: Let's see... We currently have Herman & Franklin Caranos, Arnold Lydspor, Devin Mendelberg and Ramara du Croissant. And me, of course.
textByHashes.619199e3eff11a40dfcd6127e6d67c82eff003c4: Herman and Franklin are brothers, as you'll know. Herman's always been a fishing nut. Franklin's not all that bothered about fishing, but he does like tinkering with machinery. They're originally from East Ardougne.
textByHashes.4ff72c169a2b9e8d58960498c7ab23a53feb39c5: Arnold's a bit more of a mystery. We think he might be from one of the barbarian tribes, but he never talks about that. He's always up for a chat, if you can understand his accent.
textByHashes.f420dcc38e6c0995e9008966fc2858f8447979d7: Devin Mendelberg comes from some town in Morytania - ghastly place! I'm not surprised he moved about as far west as he could go! He helps Franklin with the machinery, among other things.
textByHashes.ea7f3981f98106e4f9e99eeefc400a993e01f080: Ramara's another Ardougnian. Her father's a baker there, but she didn't want to spend her whole life baking and doing his accounts. Also, you probably know what Ardougne's crime rate is like - it can't be easy running
textByHashes.1bfd54f6436589bc7034b4e3665406170383c82a: a market stall in that city! I went there once, and some wannabe adventurer started following me around trying to pick my pockets! I kept hitting him, but he kept coming back again and again!
textByHashes.775757d091fc967b780d0a4a384d8860befa8480: Oh, I used to work with my sister, Emily, in the Rising Sun in Falador, but I wanted an outdoor job. The pay's better out here too, and the work's not too bad.
textByHashes.2e0612739cd8c5355c8c68e35919098bfa3ab2ee: You can fish! But I'd be very glad if you could go and do something about the skeletal magi in the dormitories. They've kept appearing there ever since the trolls left.
textByHashes.c049a1cc47e51506508f71b0f31482a072042f5b: Also, if you're into glassbowing, you'll probably want a bit of seaweed. It catches in our nets all the time, so you'll usually find some in the machinery down by the shore.
textByHashes.d2792088af6b315304085193d4494e2f0593186c: Ok, but come and talk to me anytime.
